branch: South-Central Dravidian (distinct from Tamil's South Dravidian)
reason_no_rate: No UD/annotated Gondi treebank exists; CGNetSwara parallel corpus has no morpheme segmentation. A running-text stacking rate comparable to the Tamil/Vedic gold numbers cannot be computed without fabrication.

documented_morphology.case_stacking: stem + OBLIGATORY oblique + case (>=2 separable bound morphemes on every case-marked noun)

sources: Krishnamurti, B. 2003. The Dravidian Languages. Cambridge UP (classifies all Dravidian, incl. Gondi, as agglutinative).
Grammar of Gondi as Spoken in the Betul District. Madras Govt Press, 1919.
Moss, C.F. 1950. An Introduction to the Grammar of the Gondi Language.
Pagdi, S.R. 1954. A Grammar of the Gondi Language.
placement: agglutinative pole (same side as Tamil, opposite Vedic), established from descriptive grammar, not a running-text rate
